Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players

Class PlayerAdapter

java.lang.Object
com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players.PlayerAdapter
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPlayer
Direct Known Subclasses:
HumanPlayer, MachinePlayer
public abstract class PlayerAdapter extends Object implements IPlayer
Adapter class that provides a complete default implementation of IPlayer for any Cincuentazo player.

Subclasses (HumanPlayer, MachinePlayer) extend this class instead of implementing IPlayer directly, so they only need to override the behavior that is specific to them. This follows the adapter pattern: the interface defines the full contract, and this abstract class absorbs every method that is identical for all players.

The hand is backed by an ArrayList, the second of the three required non-array data structures. A fixed-capacity list is appropriate here because the hand size is bounded (always 0–4 cards) and random-index access is needed when the human selects a card by position.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros

  • Field Details

    • MAX_HAND_SIZE

      public static final int MAX_HAND_SIZE
      Maximum number of cards a player may hold at any time.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerAdapter

      protected PlayerAdapter(String name)
      Constructs an PlayerAdapter with the given name and an empty hand.
      Parameters:
      name - the display name of this player; must not be null or blank
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is blank

  • Method Details

    • playCard

      public Card playCard(int cardIndex, int currentSum) throws InvalidPlayException
      Plays the card at the given hand index onto the table.

      The implementing class must verify that the card is legal before removing it from the hand. If the play is illegal it must throw InvalidPlayException and leave the hand unchanged.

      Verifies the play is legal before removing the card from the hand. The hand is not modified if the play is rejected.

      Specified by:
      playCard in interface IPlayer
      Parameters:
      cardIndex - zero-based index of the card to play
      currentSum - the running table sum before this play
      Returns:
      the Card that was played and removed from the hand
      Throws:
      InvalidPlayException - if the card would push the sum above 50
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if cardIndex is out of range

    • receiveCard

      public void receiveCard(Card card)
      Adds a card drawn from the deck to this player's hand.
      Specified by:
      receiveCard in interface IPlayer
      Parameters:
      card - the Card to receive; must not be null
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the hand is already at MAX_HAND_SIZE

    • getHand

      public List<Card> getHand()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the player's current hand.
      Specified by:
      getHand in interface IPlayer
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of the hand; the underlying list is modified by playCard(int, int) and receiveCard(com.trifasico.x50zo.model.Card)

    • hasPlayableCard

      public boolean hasPlayableCard(int currentSum)
      Returns whether the player has at least one card that can legally be played given the current table sum.
      Specified by:
      hasPlayableCard in interface IPlayer
      Parameters:
      currentSum - the running table sum
      Returns:
      true if at least one card in hand is playable

    • assertCanPlay

      public void assertCanPlay(int currentSum) throws NoPlayableCardException
      Checks that the player can move, throwing if they cannot.

      Convenience method used by the turn manager before attempting IPlayer.playCard(int, int). If the player has no legal move the exception triggers the elimination flow.

      Specified by:
      assertCanPlay in interface IPlayer
      Parameters:
      currentSum - the running table sum
      Throws:
      NoPlayableCardException - (checked) if no card in hand is playable

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the player's display name.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface IPlayer
      Returns:
      the name; never null or empty

    • handSize

      public int handSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently in the player's hand.
      Specified by:
      handSize in interface IPlayer
      Returns:
      hand size (0–4 during normal play)

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a log-friendly summary of this player's current hand.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      string such as "Player1 [A-hearts, K-spades, 7-clubs, 2-diamonds]"
